RULING:

Off Calendar Jul-09-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Vanthara Meak of HOWARD ROME MARTIN & RIDLEY LLP (650.365.7715; x202), appearing for defendant/moving party Parex USA, Inc.

Kimberly Chu of BRAYTON PURCELL LLP (415.898.1555), appearing for planitiffs/opposing parties Norma Murray, as Successor in Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Joseph Murray, Deceased; and Timothy Murray and James Murray, as Wrongful Death Heirs of Joseph Murray, Deceased.

RULING:
DEFENDANT PAREX USA, INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Matter called, no argument held. The Court adopted the tentative ruling: Defendant Parex USA, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Murray was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to paragraph 12 of Tod Kingsland's declaration are sustained and their objections to the Paul Lagier deposition testimony submitted with defendant's reply are sustained. The order denying the summary judgment motion was signed in open court. The Court declined to sign the two proposed orders regarding plaintiffs' objections stating that it was adopting the rulings on the objections as set forth in its' tentative ruling. Counsel stipulated, no objections were raised by either counsel. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Court Reporter: Kimberly Hendershott of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12552 (Tel: 877-451-1580). (503/TLJ)
